title: BluMind Benchmark — Ideal Response schema v1.0
description: Canonical 'gold-quality' response written or curated by a senior domain expert for a single case. Used as the assistant turn of `sft.jsonl` in BluMind's commercial SFT dataset. NOT a benchmark scoring artifact: the file `golds/<case>_gold.md` remains the evaluative framework (multiple accepted hypotheses/actions); an `ideal_response` is a single concrete answer that satisfies that framework and is suitable as a training target. Source of truth lives under `ideal_responses/v1.0/<case_id>_ideal.json`. The schema is public for buyer transparency, but the file contents are intentionally omitted from the public benchmark export by `scripts/export_public.py`.

benchmark_version.description: Benchmark version (e.g. 'v1.0'). Must match the case's benchmark_version.

ideal_version.description: Semver of this ideal response (e.g. '1.0.0'). Patch bumps for typo/wording fixes; minor bumps for content changes that preserve the main_hypothesis; major bumps for fundamental rewrites.

status.description: Lifecycle: 'draft' = first redaction by a single author, not yet published; 'review' = under review by a second senior expert; 'approved' = signed off by ≥2 experts and eligible for commercial export.

authors.description: Anonymized identifiers of the senior experts who authored or curated this ideal response (e.g. ['exp_01'], ['exp_01', 'exp_02']). v1.0 accepts any non-empty string; v2.0 will bind these to an expert registry with verifiable credentials.

source_strategy.description: How the ideal response was produced. 'expert-authored' = written from scratch by ≥1 expert; 'expert-curated-from-llm' = top frontier LLM response refined by an expert; 'expert-pair-*' variants require ≥2 experts (author + independent reviewer). The strategy used affects the commercial pricing tier.

seed_response_ref.description: Required when source_strategy starts with 'expert-curated-from-llm'. Identifies the LLM response that served as the seed for expert curation. Forbidden when source_strategy is 'expert-authored' or 'expert-pair-authored' (those are scratch-written).

created_at.description: When the first 'draft' was committed, in UTC ISO 8601.

last_updated_at.description: When the file was last edited, in UTC ISO 8601. Optional for status='draft'; required after the first 'review' transition.

main_hypothesis.description: Most likely technical root cause, in a single sentence. Must satisfy the gold's accepted_main_hypotheses framework.

confidence.description: Subjective confidence (0-100) — calibrated to expert experience, not LLM stochastic confidence. Typically 75-95 for an approved ideal response.

key_signals.description: Signals from the case statement that support the hypothesis. Must include all of the gold's non_ignorable_signals; should cover most of expected_key_signals.

alternatives.description: Technically plausible rival hypotheses with justification. Ideal responses must list at least 2 alternatives covering different failure mechanisms; this is stricter than the response schema (which requires ≥1) to make ideal responses richer training targets.

requested_data.description: 'Trigger' datum that would most reduce uncertainty. Must match one of the gold's accepted_requested_data entries.

recommended_action.description: Concrete operational action for the next hours/days. Must satisfy one of the gold's accepted_recommended_actions and must NOT match any of the gold's case_specific_automatic_fails (critical or recoverable).

decision_change_condition.description: Threshold or counterfactual signal under which the recommendation would change.

expert_chain_of_thought.description: PREMIUM / SFT-tier field — OPTIONAL. Free-form first-person narrative (5-15 sentences) of how the expert arrived at the answer: what they looked at first, what they discarded and why, what mental thresholds they applied, what would have changed their mind. This is the most valuable training material for teaching a model to reason like a senior RO engineer. INTENTIONALLY OMITTED from the public benchmark export by `scripts/export_public.py`. Required by `scripts/export_commercial.py` for inclusion in the high-tier SFT export.
